task_completion.description: Access the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser or application displaying the Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions database (cancer-druginteractions.org or iChart).

task_completion.description: Search for Sunitinib and the three antihypertensive drugs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Sunitinib selected as the cancer drug, and Diltiazem, Ramipril, and Bisoprolol entered or selected in the co-medications list.

task_completion.description: View the interaction traffic light colors for the selected drugs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the interaction results page showing the specific traffic light colors (e.g., Amber, Yellow, Green, or Red) for Sunitinib combined with Diltiazem, Ramipril, and Bisoprolol.

task_completion.description: Create or open the required text report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor app open with the file named 'sunitinib_bp_report.txt' (ideally showing the /sdcard/ path in the save dialog or title bar).

task_completion.description: Write the cancer drug header in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact text 'Cancer Drug: Sunitinib' written at the top of the text file.

task_completion.description: Write the interaction colors for all three drugs in the specified format
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Diltiazem: [Color]', 'Ramipril: [Color]', and 'Bisoprolol: [Color]' in the text file, where [Color] is replaced by the traffic light colors observed in the database.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual database to find the answers
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that screenshots show the agent navigating the Liverpool Cancer iChart interface and viewing the results, rather than hallucinating the colors from memory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GUI text editor to create the report
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the report was typed into a graphical text editor application, not generated using terminal commands like 'echo' or 'cat'.

integrity.description: Verify the reported colors match the database results
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the colors written in the text file match the actual traffic light colors displayed on the iChart results screen during the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database uses a traffic light system. Sunitinib + Diltiazem is a 'Potential Interaction' (typically Amber). Sunitinib + Bisoprolol is a 'Potential Weak Interaction' (typically Yellow). Sunitinib + Ramipril is not documented/no interaction expected (typically Green or unlisted). The VLM must verify that the agent actually checked these interactions and that the colors written in the report match what was displayed on the screen.
